in what world does it affect you if someone transitions to become the other gender, and becomes happier for it? <@940306371625250826>
I also think they should have treated people better when this stuff first started, my first interaction with a trans person I was verbally harassed because I used the wrong pronouns

firstly: trans people dont claim to change their sex, but rather their gender identity. These are two separate things. Their personal belief is that they belong to the other gender. Medical interventions such as hrt dont aim to rewrite DNA but instead aim to align the trans persons own identity, making them more happy in their own body. I personally dont see anything wrong with this, as this is a completely normal thing to seek after if you are trans, as this will bring them joy. They only ask that you call them by their correct pronouns, which many people such as you purposefully disrupt and disallow their personal joy through simply excluding them. Additionally, the medical research priorities directed towards trans people are simply a small fraction of the overall medical resources available, and many of these are going to other important causes. There are even studies to back up that trans people will be happier with their gender, such as how trans people with access to gender affirming care are 50% less likely to end their own life than those who dont have access. I personally feel like trans people are not demanding acceptance, but rather simply asking for respect in a world as toxic as ours, as seen with people like you. Obviously this is just my take, and everyone has alternate takes, but ive met some really cool people that are trans and i dont think they deserve to get mistreated

i believe that there should be a set time period before people fully decide they are trans, as otherwise can lead to problematic complications. I agree with you there. They should seek confirmation and determine whether or not the thoughts are true, and if so they should go ahead with the surgery to make themselves happier. I personally believe that even if they are trans, you should still call them by their pronouns. This is essentially telling someone that "oh i completely disagree with your beliefs", which you clearly do through not calling them their pronouns. By purposefully calling them the wrong pronouns and not listening to their beliefs you are directly dehumanizing their experience and calling it "wrong".
